digest.top_line: TREASURE: adding consolidative TRT to atezo maintenance in ES-SCLC halted early for toxicity; OS 6.7 vs 13.4mo (HR 1.55, ns), SAEs 61% vs 18%.
digest.tldr: Thoracic carried the day's only signal, and it's a cautionary one for RT: consolidative TRT (30 Gy/10 fx, CREST dosing) added to an IO backbone in ES-SCLC gave no PFS/OS benefit and a fatal-AE rate of 19% vs 3%, stopping the trial early. The IO era flips CREST's risk-benefit.

studies.name: TREASURE
studies.tldr: OS 6.7 vs 13.4mo (HR 1.55, ns), SAEs 61% vs 18% adding consolidative TRT to atezo maintenance in ES-SCLC; stopped early for toxicity.

details.text: 📊 Efficacy by arm (TRT+atezo vs atezo alone)
📊 Safety by arm — the signal that stopped the trial

details: 🔍 Phase 2 open-label RCT, 20 sites Germany/Austria; ES-SCLC ≥SD after carbo-etoposide-atezo induction
🔍 Consolidative TRT 30 Gy/10 fx, same dose/fractionation as CREST
⚠️ SMC halted recruitment at 68/104 planned for excess fatal SAEs → underpowered for OS primary
⚠️ OS numerically worse but HR CI crosses 1 (P=.34); read is no benefit plus toxicity, not proven OS detriment
💊 Driver: infection + respiratory SAEs, tied to radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion; lower DLCO in fatal-AE pts
🔗 vs CREST (Lancet 2015, pre-IO era): modest OS benefit with same-dose TRT; adding the IO backbone flipped the risk-benefit

verdict.rationale: Randomised, prespecified OS endpoint; halted by SMC for a clear safety divergence (fatal AEs P=.04) that argues against extending CREST-era consolidative TRT into the IO era. Underpowered but valid.

studies.significance: The RT dose (30 Gy/10 fx) matches CREST, so the reversal from benefit to harm isn't a dose story: it's the IO backbone plus radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion driving infection and respiratory SAEs. Argues against routine consolidative TRT added to atezo maintenance in unselected ES-SCLC; DLCO and lymphopenia are candidate selection filters.
studies.monday_clinic: In unselected ES-SCLC with ≥SD after chemo-IO induction, this questions routinely adding consolidative thoracic RT to atezolizumab maintenance; it does not settle whether carefully selected pts (adequate DLCO, no marked lymphopenia) might still benefit.

related_trials.brief_title: Olaparib and Durvalumab With Carboplatin, Etoposide, and/or Radiation Therapy for the Treatment of Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer, PRIO Trial
related_trials.brief_summary: This phase I/II trials investigates the side effects of olaparib and durvalumab and how well it works in combination with carboplatin, etoposide, and/or radiation therapy in treating patients with extensive stage-small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) who have not received treatment for their disease. PARPs are proteins that help repair DNA mutations. PARP inhibitors, such as olaparib, can keep PARP from working, so tumor cells can't repair themselves, and they may stop growing. related_trials.brief_summary: Esophageal squamous cell carcinoma (ESCC) is one of the most aggressive malignant tumors. papers.title: Consolidative Thoracic Radiotherapy With Atezolizumab Maintenance in Extensive-Stage Small Cell Lung Cancer
papers.abstract: Importance Chemoimmunotherapy followed by immunotherapy maintenance is the standard first-line treatment for extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC), yet data regarding efficacy and safety of consolidative thoracic radiotherapy (TRT) are lacking. Objective To determine whether combining consolidative TRT with immunotherapy maintenance in ES-SCLC is safe and improves patients’ overall survival (OS) and progression-free survival (PFS). Design, Settings, and Participants This was a multicenter open-label phase 2 randomized clinical trial recruiting patients from September 2020 to August 2022 in Germany and Austria, with the last follow-up visit in September 2024. Eligible patients had ES-SCLC with at least stable disease after induction chemoimmunotherapy (carboplatin−etoposide−atezolizumab). Although the database was locked in April 2025, a post hoc survival update was performed in April 2026. Data were analyzed from April 2025 to April 2026. Interventions Randomized (1:1) to either atezolizumab maintenance combined with consolidative TRT (30 Gy in 10 fractions) (arm A) or atezolizumab maintenance alone (arm B). Main Outcome and Measure OS (time from randomization to death due to any cause). Results Of 96 patients assessed for eligibility, 68 patients were randomized; recruitment was prematurely terminated due to safety concerns. Median OS in the combination arm was numerically shorter compared to atezolizumab only (6.7 months [95% CI, 5.1-9.0] vs 13.4 months [95% CI, 10.7-17.5]; HR, 1.55 [95% CI, 0.90-2.69]; P = .34), while median PFS was similar (2.4 months [95% CI, 1.3-3.9] vs 2.6 months [95% CI, 1.2-3.9]; HR, 0.92 [95% CI, 0.54-1.55]; P = .85). TRT plus atezolizumab was accompanied by higher frequency of severe adverse events (SAEs) (19 patients [61.3%] vs 6 patients [18.2%]; P &amp;lt; .001) and fatal outcomes (6 patients [19.4%] vs 1 patient [3.0%]; P = .04). Safety analysis revealed predominance of infection and respiratory disorder−related SAEs, possibly facilitated by a depletion of lymphocytes observed specifically in patients after TRT, and by a lower single breath diffuse capacity of the lungs for carbon monoxide in patients with fatal AEs in arm A. No further risk factors were identified, given that baseline characteristics were well balanced between both arms and between patients with and without SAEs, including fatal SAEs. Conclusions and Relevance In this randomized clinical trial, TRT combined with immunotherapy increased toxic effects in unselected patients with ES-SCLC without survival benefit, presumably due to radiation-induced lymphocyte depletion facilitating infections. Cautious patient selection and further investigation to identify those who may benefit without undue risk are required. Trial Registration ClinicalTrials.gov Identifier: NCT04462276
